Class StackList<Type>

java.lang.Object
stacks.StackList<Type>
Type Parameters:
Type -
All Implemented Interfaces:
Iterable<Type>
public class StackList<Type> extends Object implements Iterable<Type>
A generic stack implementation using a list.

    Returns an iterator to traverse the stack from top to bottom.

    Peeks at the top item of the stack without removing it.

    Pops the top item off the stack and returns it.

  • Method Details

    • push

      public void push(Type item)
      Pushes an item onto the top of the stack.
      Parameters:
      item - that will be placed on top

    • pop

      public Type pop()
      Pops the top item off the stack and returns it.
      Returns:
      the item that was on top of the stack

    • peek

      public Type peek()
      Peeks at the top item of the stack without removing it.
      Returns:

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the size of the stack.
      Returns:
      number of elements in the stack

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Clears all elements from the stack.

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Checks if the stack is empty.
      Returns:
      true if stack is empty, false otherwise

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of the stack.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      string showing name and elements of the stack

    • iterator

      public Iterator<Type> iterator()
      Returns an iterator to traverse the stack from top to bottom.
      Specified by:
      iterator in interface Iterable<Type>
      Returns:
      iterator for the stack from inner class StackListIterator
